scope: Build the second internal/driver.Driver: a codex-cli subprocess driver that dispatches the implementer and verifier roles rooted at the slice worktree, over S02's shared subprocess.go plumbing, proving the Driver contract (S01) is not claude-shaped (N=2) and closing sworn#19 (S63-deferral-1).

reachability.evidence: go test ./internal/driver/... -run TestCodexDispatchImplementer -v: CodexDriver.Dispatch spawns a real subprocess (the re-exec'd test binary acting as a fake codex CLI) rooted at a real git worktree, asserts the recorded child cmd.Dir equals WorktreeRoot and the argv contains `exec --json -C <dir>`, and parses the subprocess's real JSONL stdout stream into Result (ResultText="done", CostUSD=0, CostSource=provider-reported, InputTokens=100, OutputTokens=50, ModelID falls back to the requested model, DurationMS falls back to the measured wall-clock) — the full Dispatch path end-to-end through its actual subprocess boundary, not a leaf-level mock. TestCodexDispatchVerifier (AC-02, asserts --ephemeral + VerdictSchema in the prompt), TestCodexWorktreeGate (Rule 11: asserts the marker-writing fake was never invoked when AssertWorktree fails), and TestCodexErrorMapping (AC-03, table-driven timeout/missing-binary/non-zero-exit) exercise the same integration point for the remaining acceptance checks. Real-CLI (not fake-binary) integration proof is explicitly out of scope for this slice (spec.json out_of_scope, and R-01) and deferred to S10's SIT smoke + the Rule-10 cutover journey walk.

delivered.item: AC-01: implementer dispatch spawns `codex exec --json -C <dir>` with cmd.Dir=WorktreeRoot, JSONL stream normalized into Result
AC-02: verifier dispatch adds --ephemeral, includes VerdictSchema in the prompt, populates StructuredJSON when the final agent_message parses as a JSON object, ErrKind=protocol otherwise

AC-03: non-zero exit -> auth (binding cross-driver contract, S02 pin 2 — not spec.json's stale 'provider' parenthetical, tracked sworn#84), timeout -> transient, missing binary -> config, table-driven; never a panic or half-populated ok Result
AC-04: GOCACHE/GOMODCACHE redirected outside the worktree, HOME left untouched (shared hygieneEnv, unchanged); missing usage degrades to CostUSD=0/CostSource=unknown, never fabricated
AC-05: go test ./internal/driver/... passes, including S02's claude-driver tests unchanged (shared-plumbing regression guard)
delivered.evidence: go test ./internal/driver/... -v output — 30 tests, all PASS, TestClaude* unmodified

delivered.item: Design decision 6 (Type-1, pre-ratified): codex non-zero-exit ErrKind is ErrKindAuth, per the binding cross-driver contract from S02's pin 2 — recorded, not a fresh judgement call
spawnClassified split: subprocess.go generalised to parameterize the non-zero-exit ErrKind without touching claude.go or any existing claude/subprocess test
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/subprocess.go (spawn() delegates ErrKindAuth to spawnClassified); go test ./internal/driver/... TestClaude*/TestSpawn_* all still pass unmodified

delivered.item: Rule 2 deferral: spec.json AC-03's 'provider' parenthetical is stale text (pre-ratification carry-over), corrected via journal.md + tracker, not silently worked around
